selectionRule: A candidate is eligible only when an external organisation owns the implementation and oracle, the surface addresses a documented consumer problem, and results are attributable and reproducible.

candidates.boundedSurface: Consume the versioned PIC trace, missingness, or fixture-converter contract in a clean upstream test job without changing PolicyEngine calculation semantics.
candidates.expectedEffort: One small adapter or fixture corpus plus an upstream test workflow.
candidates.independenceRisk: A maintainer-controlled fork or contributor branch is insufficient unless the implementation and oracle are independently curated and the external organisation acknowledges the result.
candidates.noResponseExit: After one authorized issue/PR follow-up and the documented response window, record unresponsive and do not count the candidate toward v1 adoption.

candidates.boundedSurface: Validate a read-only PIC trace or missingness projection against OpenFisca's existing calculation and test conventions, preserving OpenFisca as the semantic engine.
candidates.expectedEffort: One adapter fixture set and a reproducible upstream CI invocation.
candidates.independenceRisk: The RaC maintainer cannot curate the OpenFisca oracle or treat an unreviewed local fork as independent evidence.
candidates.noResponseExit: After one authorized follow-up and the documented response window, record unresponsive and do not count the candidate toward v1 adoption.

candidates.boundedSurface: Run the PIC fixture and trace projection against the NZ rules package used by the existing reconciliation study, without promoting any agent-generated fixture as golden evidence.
candidates.expectedEffort: One NZ-specific adapter fixture set and a clean hosted test run.
candidates.independenceRisk: The existing local fork and reconciliation study are contributor-controlled and therefore rehearsal evidence only until BetterRules independently runs and acknowledges the result.
candidates.noResponseExit: After one authorized follow-up and the documented response window, record unresponsive and do not count the candidate toward v1 adoption.

candidates.boundedSurface: Use the self-contained PIC implementer kit with an independently curated synthetic process case and publish the input, output, oracle, environment, and checksums.
candidates.expectedEffort: A bounded clean-room implementation and one reproducible evidence packet.
candidates.owner: Dylan to nominate; external organisation to execute
candidates.independenceRisk: The organisation, implementation, and oracle curator must be independent of this repository and must acknowledge the result; no candidate has yet been identified.
candidates.noResponseExit: If no suitable organisation is identified after the approved outreach round, record no-selection and keep the v1 gate blocked rather than substituting an internal rehearsal.

humanBoundary: No candidate is approved, contacted, submitted, or counted as adoption by this registry. Human authorization is required for each external communication.
sourceOfTruth: This registry supplements CANDIDATES.md; external acknowledgements and immutable result packets must be added before any candidate can satisfy the v1 gate.
